differences.detail: Production is a tight single-column mobile stack with compact vertical rhythm; prototype is a wide modal with larger paddings/margins and more horizontal composition.
Prototype uses larger and heavier typography (titles, metrics, CTA), while production text is smaller, denser, and more uniform across cards.
Prototype introduces stronger red accents and brighter glow highlights; production is more muted navy/cyan with softer accent contrast.
Prototype has larger corner radii (outer modal and section cards), while production corners are tighter and more consistent across list cards.
Prototype relies on stronger shadows/glows for depth; production appears flatter with subtler elevation and emphasis.
Player and emblem visuals are significantly larger in prototype (especially roster portraits), whereas production uses smaller thumbnails and denser list-item imagery.
Production prioritizes 'recent 10', next match, then roster in a strict vertical flow; prototype spreads emphasis across roster, tables, hero/stats split, and CTA, changing scan order.

top_3_fixes: Match production structure first: convert prototype to compact single-column mobile flow, reduce section paddings, and tighten vertical spacing.
Normalize visual style tokens: reduce red/glow intensity, align to production navy/cyan accent balance, and lower shadow strength.
Adjust component scale: shrink portraits/icons and typography to production proportions, then rebalance heading/body/value hierarchy.
